sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
An ulcer on his right foot that has been present for approximately six to eight weeks.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports the ulcer developed approximately six to eight weeks ago after stepping on a nail in the backyard. He describes the ulcer as being present on his right foot and has not been going away. He notes that the ulcer has appeared to worsen since its initial appearance. The patient reports occasional numbness in his feet, particularly in colder weather. He also reports frequent tension headaches. He quit smoking approximately four years ago, having smoked at least two packs per day for twenty years. He is currently managing his diabetes with insulin and reports that his sugars have been running higher than normal. He denies any chest pain or shortness of breath.
Findings

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Physical examination reveals a two by three centimeter ulcerated lesion on the right lateral foot near the fifth metatarsal-metatarsophalangeal joint. There is no associated cellulitis. The wound demonstrates granulation tissue and some purulent discharge. Vital signs are unremarkable: blood pressure is 127/78 mmHg, heart rate is 62 beats per minute, and temperature is 98.6°F. Dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses are palpable. Jugular venous distension and carotid bruits are absent. Lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally. Heart sounds are normal with a two out of six systolic ejection murmur heard at the left base.
Assessment
A non-healing ulcer of the right foot, likely secondary to underlying diabetic neuropathy and vascular insufficiency.
Plan
Order an arterial ultrasound of the lower extremities to assess vascular supply. A podiatry consult will be arranged for wound care management. Continue aspirin daily. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to assess wound healing and adjust treatment as needed. Refer to a vascular surgeon for consideration of potential interventions, including stent placement or bypass surgery, if indicated by the arterial ultrasound results. Coordinate with the primary care physician to optimize diabetes management and control.
medical_concepts: non-healing ulcer of the right foot
non-healing ulcer of the right foot
